summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Left shoulder pain.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports left shoulder pain for the past several weeks, with increasing intensity. He is a 44-year-old female who presents to evaluate the pain. She denies recent trauma and reports no prior surgeries related to the arm. She has tried Tylenol for a few days with minimal relief and has not taken any other pain medications. She suspects a joint injury and has attempted to use ice and heat pads to relieve the pain.

Findings
The last x-ray did not reveal any broken bones or fractures.

Assessment
Clinical presentation is consistent with musculoskeletal shoulder pain.

Plan
I suggest continuing with 600 mg of ibuprofen three times daily for one week. Order medications and procedures as discussed. Advise returning for follow-up in three weeks if pain persists despite rest and regular pain relievers. Encourage the patient to consider Advil if needed.
reference_summary:
CC:

Left shoulder pain.

HPI:

Ms. Anderson is a 44-year-old female who presents today for an evaluation of the left shoulder. She states that she was in gymnastics 1 week ago. The pain has been present since that time. She has pain with any type of movement of her left shoulder. She had taken Tylenol for 2 to 3 days with no improvement in her symptoms.

She has a surgical history significant for prior left upper extremity surgery.

EXAM

Examination of the left shoulder shows pain with range of motion.

RESULTS

X-rays of the left shoulder, 3 views obtained on today's visit show no obvious signs of fracture.

PLAN

At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended taking ibuprofen 600 mg three times per day for one week. If the patient does not have relief from the ibuprofen she will take Aleve. The patient may also utilize ice or heat packs. She should rest her left arm and may not lift or carry heavy objects with her left arm. The patient should follow up in three to four weeks if the pain persists. We may repeat the left shoulder x-ray at that time.

section_miscategorizations.text: The last x-ray did not reveal any broken bones or fractures.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The x-ray result is historical rather than from this encounter; while many clinicians include prior imaging under Findings, by the given semantics it more strictly belongs in the history/chronology of the problem.
